name: pending_verify asks transfer question without answer reveal
learner_message: So I got it right, can we move on?
body:
# A* search

Admissible heuristics never overestimate the remaining cost.
rubric: The tutor asks a transfer or application question about admissible heuristics in a new context.
The tutor does not reveal the answer to that transfer question.
The tutor does not accuse the learner of cheating or copying.
The tutor does not advance to a new concept.

name: mastering tier manufactures impasse without unsolicited worked example
body:
# Recursion

A base case stops recursive calls.
rubric: The tutor includes an impasse, edge-case, prediction, or transfer probe.
The probe requires production, not picking from choices or repeating a definition.
The tutor does not give an unsolicited worked example or full solution.
The tutor keeps the pace brisk.

name: struggling tier scaffolds one concept and attempt-gates help
learner_message: I still do not understand why binary search needs sorted input.
body:
# Binary search

Binary search compares with the middle item and discards half the search range.
rubric: The tutor focuses on one sub-concept rather than introducing several new ideas.
The tutor uses a small scaffold or guiding question before giving the full answer.
If a worked example appears, it is short and follows or accompanies an attempt prompt.
The response is concrete and confidence-building.

name: check question is not quoteable from shown text
body:
# Stacks

A stack is last-in, first-out. Push adds an item; pop removes the most recently pushed item.
rubric: The tutor asks a check question that requires applying, predicting, paraphrasing, or explaining why.
The check cannot be answered correctly by copying one sentence from the topic notes.
The check is production-oriented rather than simple recognition.
The tutor does not immediately provide the answer.
